what_went_right: Pure module design: lib/focus.js is fully headless (takes a `getComment` accessor, not a reference to `state`), enabling 17 unit tests that run without a browser and without app.js imports — the ancestor-walk, cycle guard, dangling-parent, and backfill-staleness contract are all pinned in code before the feature shipped (diff: new file lib/focus.js + test/focus.test.mjs).

Reviewer ran TWICE and all findings were closed before push: first pass caught the Critical + 3 Should-fix; second pass confirmed all CLOSED. The transcript shows the work-agent called code-reviewer before commit, not after (transcript: 'app.js parses cleanly and all identifiers are wired. This touches the load-bearing applySearch filter path — exactly what my pre-tag-review-gate memory says must get a review.').

The backfill-staleness regression test was added as part of the Critical fix — test/focus.test.mjs line 1191 ('a stale memo hides a row whose ancestor was backfilled — so callers MUST drop the memo when the store changes') pins the exact failure scenario in the test suite, making the fix permanent rather than honor-system.

applyThreadFilter was correctly folded into the unified applySearch intersection pass (focus ∩ thread ∩ search), eliminating a separate function that could have diverged from focus-mode visibility logic — the refactor reduced total LOC and eliminated the class of 'thread filter overwritten by search' bugs.

Memory consultation fired at session start and directly shaped strategy: betterssc-direct-to-main-no-worktree, inline-reviewer-cadence-traded-parallelism-for-correctness, and pre-tag-review-gate-is-load-bearing were all cited by the work-agent in its first assistant turn before any code was written.
what_went_wrong: The state comment at the initial memo-scope decision was wrong: 'cleared whenever focusFilter changes (appends keep it valid since new messages are leaves, never ancestors of older cached rows)' — diff line 316. The author reasoned about one mutation source (realtime appends) and missed the second (history backfill / loadOlder / bgPrefetch inserting older messages that are potential ancestors). A reviewer caught it as Critical.

The original state declaration set `_focusMemo: null` with the intention of reusing it across renders within a filter generation — but the memo's actual safe scope is ONE render pass. The design was subtly over-ambitious: cross-render caching requires enumerating ALL store mutation sources, which was not done.

The thread-only tint regression (groups that are in-thread but NOT in a search hit should still get 'search-hit' class for tinting) was not carried forward from the deleted `applyThreadFilter` into the unified pass — caught as a Should-fix by the reviewer and fixed in the same post-review pass.
three_whys: Why 1: The _focusMemo Critical happened because the work-agent scoped the memo's validity to 'realtime append' behavior only, writing 'appends keep it valid since new messages are leaves, never ancestors of older cached rows' — this is true for one mutation source but false for history backfill (diff line 316).

Why 2: The author reasoned about memo validity from the write path they were actively implementing (the realtime poll → new leaf at bottom), not from a systematic enumeration of all store mutation sources. The history backfill path (loadOlder / bgPrefetch) was a pre-existing feature not in focus during the memo design, so it didn't enter the mental model.

Why 3: There was no established discipline for 'enumerate all store mutation sources before scoping a cross-render cache' — the prior project memories cover symmetric invariants in send/retry paths and filter intersection bugs, but not the specific class of 'ordered store with two growth directions + upward-walking memo'. This is a new class of error for this codebase, so no rule existed to trigger the check.

evidence_citations.quote:
+  // generation; cleared whenever focusFilter changes (appends keep it valid
+  // since new messages are leaves, never ancestors of older cached rows).

+  // it must NOT survive across renders. History backfill (loadOlder /
+  // bgPrefetch) inserts OLDER messages that can be ancestors of rows we
+  // already evaluated; a memo that persisted would keep their stale
+  // pre-backfill verdict
+  it("a stale memo hides a row whose ancestor was backfilled — so callers MUST drop the memo when the store changes", () => {
The reviewer caught a real Critical — exactly the highest-risk claim I flagged. My 'new messages are leaves' reasoning holds for realtime appends at the bottom but is WRONG for history backfill

action_items.title: Add discipline: enumerate all store mutation sources before scoping any cross-render cache
action_items.description: Before writing a memo/cache over a store structure, list ALL code paths that mutate the store (realtime append, history backfill, bgPrefetch, optimistic reconcile, etc.) and verify the cache invariant holds for each. If ANY source can insert ancestors of already-cached nodes, scope the cache to one render pass only. Apply this check to any future tree-walk memos (thread depth, quote chains, etc.) in app.js.
